comprehensive_summary:
Alfred Jarry was a French absurdist playwright and author, widely considered a forefather of Absurdism. He is overwhelmingly identified through his play Ubu Roi, a scatological send-up of Macbeth in which the cowardly title character usurps the throne of Poland and goes to war with Russia. The play's premiere caused an uproar when the title character exclaimed "merde!" (with an extra R), leading Jarry to later stage it with marionettes. The title character frequently swears "by my green candle." W.B. Yeats, who attended the premiere, lamented "after us, the Savage God." Paul Sérusier and Pierre Bonnard helped design the set. The play ends with the title character fleeing to France to escape Bougrelas and the Tsar's army. Bougrelas and Bordure avenge the death of King Wenceslas of Poland by deposing the title character. Sequels titled Ubu Cuckolded and Ubu in Chains followed. Rachilde, a French Decadent author, was a close friend of Jarry and helped establish the myth about the riotous opening night.

Jarry also created a significant body of visual art. A woodcut by Jarry depicts one of his characters (Ubu) in a dunce cap with a spiral affixed to his bloated stomach. The Beraldi Album includes his sketch of two men clapping before a burning house. His work influenced several paintings: a painting reimagines Ubu as a monstrous red-bricked spinning top with a green scarf, titled with the word "imperator"; he influenced the inclusion of a doctor's peephole in the painting The Addition and of a wispy crown adorned with "IMRA" in Portrait of Madame B; a photograph inspired by Jarry depicting a close-up of an armadillo fetus was captured by Dora Maar; and a painting inspired by him features a thin metal scythe balancing on its tip in sand.

Jarry invented the "science of imaginary solutions" called 'pataphysics, which he described as a field that "explains the universe supplementary to this one." His picaresque novel Exploits and Opinions of Dr. Faustroll features the laughing baboon Bosse-de-Nage as companion to a doctor of 'pataphysics. He also wrote The Passion Considered as an Uphill Bicycle Race.

works.name: Ubu Roi
works.description: Jarry's most famous work, a scatological absurdist play that sends up Macbeth. The title character usurps the throne of Poland and goes to war with Russia. Its premiere caused a scandal over the opening word "merde" (with an extra R).

clues.clue: The title character exclaims "merde!" (with an extra R) at the premiere, causing an uproar; later staged with marionettes
examples: This author started staging one of his plays with marionettes after an uproar at its premiere over the title character's exclamation of 'merde!' with an extra R.
Rachilde established the myth about the riotous opening night of this author's play Ubu Roi.

clues.clue: The title character swears "by my green candle"
examples: This play's protagonist frequently swears 'by my green candle.'
In this absurdist play, a cowardly king swears 'by my green candle.'

clues.clue: A send-up of Macbeth; the title king usurps the throne of Poland and goes to war with Russia
examples: Sequels titled for a man 'Cuckolded' and 'in Chains' follow a send-up of Macbeth in which the title king goes to war with Russia after he usurps the throne of Poland.

clues.clue: W.B. Yeats lamented "after us, the Savage God" after seeing the play
examples: W.B. Yeats lamented, 'after us, the Savage God' after seeing a play by this author.

clues.clue: Paul Sérusier and Pierre Bonnard helped design the set for the premiere
examples: Paul Serusier and Pierre Bonnard helped design the set for the premiere of what author's play Ubu Roi?

clues.clue: Ends with the title character fleeing to France; Bougrelas and Bordure avenge King Wenceslas's death
examples: This play ends with the title character fleeing to France to escape Bougrelas and the Tsar's army.
Bougrelas and Bordure avenge the death of King Wenceslas of Poland by deposing the title character.

clues.clue: Sequels titled Ubu Cuckolded and Ubu in Chains
examples: Sequels titled for a man 'Cuckolded' and 'in Chains' follow.

clues.clue: Described as a play "for actors pretending to be puppets"
examples: This play 'for actors pretending to be puppets' ends with the title character fleeing to France.

clues.clue: Jarry created a woodcut of Ubu in a dunce cap with a spiral on his bloated stomach
examples: A woodcut created by this author depicts one of his characters in a dunce cap with a spiral affixed to his bloated stomach.

clues.clue: A painting reimagines Ubu as a monstrous red-bricked spinning top with a green scarf, titled "imperator"
examples: A green scarf adorns this author's best-known creation in a painting that reimagines him as a monstrous, red-bricked spinning top and whose title calls him 'imperator.'

works.name: Exploits and Opinions of Dr. Faustroll / 'Pataphysics
works.description: A picaresque novel featuring the laughing baboon Bosse-de-Nage as companion to a doctor of 'pataphysics, a field Jarry invented as the "science of imaginary solutions" that "explains the universe supplementary to this one."

clues.clue: Invented 'pataphysics, the "science of imaginary solutions" that "explains the universe supplementary to this one"
examples: This author invented the 'science of imaginary solutions,' 'pataphysics.
This proponent of 'Pataphysics is considered a forefather of Absurdism.

clues.clue: The picaresque novel features the laughing baboon Bosse-de-Nage as companion to a doctor of 'pataphysics
examples: A picaresque novel by this author features the laughing baboon Bosse-de-Nage as a companion to a doctor of a field that 'explains the universe supplementary to this one.'

works.name: Visual Art and Influence on Artists
works.description: Jarry influenced numerous visual artists. His Beraldi Album includes a sketch of two men clapping before a burning house. He influenced paintings including The Addition (doctor's peephole), Portrait of Madame B (wispy crown with "IMRA"), and a Dora Maar photograph of an armadillo fetus.

clues.clue: Influenced The Addition (doctor's peephole) and Portrait of Madame B (wispy crown with "IMRA")
examples: This author influenced the inclusion of a doctor's peephole in the painting The Addition and of a wispy crown adorned with the letters 'IMRA' in Portrait of Madame B.

clues.clue: The Beraldi Album includes his sketch of two men clapping before a burning house
examples: The Beraldi Album includes this author's sketch of two men clapping before a burning house.

clues.clue: Dora Maar captured a photograph of an armadillo fetus inspired by Jarry
examples: A photograph inspired by this author that depicts a close-up of an armadillo fetus was captured by Dora Maar.

clues.clue: A painting inspired by Jarry features a thin metal scythe balancing on its tip in sand
examples: On the right side of a painting inspired by this author, a thin metal scythe precariously balances on its tip in the sand.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: French absurdist playwright considered a forefather of Absurdism. Close friends with Rachilde, a French Decadent author. Also wrote The Passion Considered as an Uphill Bicycle Race.

examples: His other works include The Passion Considered as an Uphill Bicycle Race.
